steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains that difrasismo meanings seem obscure because semantic relations are "tenuous," but become intelligible through conceptual associations in Aztec ceremonial culture. The right answer must capture both the obscurity and its cultural resolution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Its frequency in Classical Nahuatl poetry confirms its intelligibility to the Aztec audience.: Introduces an unsupported claim about how often difrasismos appear. ✗
  • Choice B — Its unintelligibility may cause its formal function within a line of verse to go unnoticed by present-day readers.: Brings in modern readers and a formal poetic function not discussed in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — Its apparent obscurity can be resolved when considered in the proper cultural context.: Matches exactly — the passage says relations are tenuous yet become clear through Aztec ceremonial associations. ✓
  • Choice D — Its metaphorical significance derives from the semantic equivalence of the two nouns constituting the difrasismo.: Contradicts the passage, which explicitly calls the semantic relations "tenuous," not equivalent. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
